titleStmt.title: Picture Postcard from Roger Casement to Robert Monteith, 23 February 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Roger Casement

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Roger Casement, (1864-1916), Irish nationalist, arrested at Banna Strand, Co. Kerry on Good Friday 1916 while trying to land arms secured in Germany. He was tried in the Old Bailey for treason and subsequently executed by hanging at Pentonville Prison. Robert Monteith – member of the Irish Republican Brotherhood, associate of Roger Casement engaged in recruitment of Irish Prisoners of War in Europe for the Irish Volunteers. Accompanied Casement on the ill-fated voyage which ended at Banna Strand, Co. Kerry on Good Friday, 1916. This is a receipt for 2,050 German Marks which Casement withdrew from funds held in the bank under Monteith's name, and an account of how he used the money.
The front of the Postcard has a picture of the Hotel Saxonia, Potsdamer Platz, Berlin, where Casement is known to have stayed.

msIdentifier.collection: Bureau of Military History Contemporary Documents, Brother Allen Collection BMH CD/75/2/1

correspAction.placeName: Hotel Saxonia, Potsdamer Platz, Berlin, Germany

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Hotel Saxonia, Berlin W9
Königgrätzerstr. 10
Budapester Str.10 23 Feby 1916 My dear Mr. Monteith, Please regard this
as a receipt for the Marks 2050.
I handed me yesterday from the funds in
your name at the Bank. The refund of
MKs 1750 I gave my McFadden to get
away and 250 300 MKs repaid by yourself
I return to Munich tonight I hope & does
this to cover your refund. Yours
Roger Casement
Potsdamer Bahnhof Potsdamer Platz Hotel Saxonia
